data.title: TAL Education Group posted strong Q3 results with net revenues of $770.2M (+27%), net income at $130.6M (+466%), & cash of $3,617.4M.
data.description: TAL Education Group, a smart learning provider in China, reported its unaudited financial results for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026. In this period, net revenues were US$770.2 million, up 27.0% from the same period the year before, with a reported income from operations of US$93.1 million. The non-GAAP income from operations, excluding share-based compensation expenses, was US$104.0 million. Net income attributable to TAL was US$130.6 million, while non-GAAP net income attributable to TAL reached US$141.4 million. For the first nine months ended November 30, 2025, net revenues were US$2,206.5 million, with income from operations of US$203.6 million. Non-GAAP net income attributable to TAL was US$319.3 million for this period. TAL's cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled US$3,617.4 million as of November 30, 2025. TAL's financial performance showed growth, with a focus on integrating technology into learning experiences and enhancing content, products, and services. TAL's strategic initiatives aim to build competitive advantages and generate value for users and society.

data.title: New study by BCG, Heidrick & INSEAD shows boards in emerging markets facing higher uncertainty, improving governance.
data.description: A recent worldwide research project delves into the ways in which boards in developing economies are enhancing governance, resilience, and decision-making in the face of increasing uncertainty and complexity. The collaboration between Boston Consulting Group (BCG) and Heidrick & Struggles, a top-notch provider, has produced insightful findings. The study was released on January 29, 2026, in London. The report sheds light on how boards are adapting to challenges in the current landscape.

data.title: Apollo refinances Pan-European logistics and industrial portfolio with €900M for Cerberus and Arrow Capital Partners.
data.description: A customized and secure senior financial solution has been implemented to refinance a high-quality portfolio, aimed at consolidating the base of lenders involved. This initiative is focused on improving the financial position by addressing the outstanding debt related to the portfolio. The refinancing operation involves significant capital, positioning it as a strategic move to enhance the overall stability and effectiveness of the financial structure. The refinancing action is expected to have a substantial impact on the financial outlook and strengthen the relationship with lenders.

data.title: Invenio Imaging names Adrian Whitford as Independent Board Director and adds Biao He & John Seamans to its Board.
data.description: A company based in Santa Clara, California called Invenio Imaging, known for its advancements in fresh tissue imaging and artificial intelligence, has appointed Adrian Whitford as an Independent Board Director on January 29, 2026. This new appointment follows the addition of Biao He from Oak BioCapital and John Seamans to the board. Invenio Imaging continues to expand its board of directors to include experienced individuals, with Whitford now joining to contribute to the company's growth and strategic decisions.

data.title: MCO starts 2026 strong with 22% revenue growth from 2024 to 2025 and onboarding 100+ new clients.
data.description: In January 29, 2026, MCO (MyComplianceOffice) shared that they have had a successful beginning to 2026, following a remarkable 2025 characterized by expansion, acknowledgment in the industry, and ongoing enhancements to their platform. The company reported a substantial 22% rise in revenue from 2024 to 2025, along with the successful onboarding of over 100 new...

data.title: BIO-key EMEA partners with Citadel Global to expand in India, enhance biometric identity solutions for EU–India trade agreement.
data.description: EMEA branch of BIO-key collaborates with Citadel Global to strengthen their footprint in India and drive the adoption of identity solutions conducive to the EU-India trade agreement. The partnership aims at enhancing market presence and leveraging opportunities in India. This move signifies a strategic approach towards capturing a larger market share in India, a key player in the trade agreement, boosting the adoption of identity solutions.

data.title: LifeArc Ventures expands in 2025 with investments in four companies, marking its most active year.
data.description: In London on January 29, 2026, it was reported that LifeArc Ventures, the venture capital division of the medical research organization LifeArc, experienced its busiest year in 2025. The company made notable investments in four new cutting-edge life sciences portfolio companies, in addition to providing additional funding and support to its existing ventures. This surge in activity marked a significant increase in the company's investment portfolio, demonstrating a commitment to fostering innovation and advancement in the medical field.

data.title: West Red Lake Gold reports high-grade continuity at Rowan project with results from 7 drill holes to enhance the resource.
data.description: West Red Lake Gold has released findings from the initial 7 drill holes of their drilling initiative at the Rowan project. The program aims to enhance the resource potential through targeted efforts.

data.title: Trump's ban on institutional homebuyers could impact Blackstone, though it has minor exposure with institutional ownership at 0.5%.
data.description: President Donald Trump's recent proposal to limit institutional purchases of single-family homes has raised concerns within the real estate sector and for alternative asset managers like Blackstone Inc. (NYSE: BX). Blackstone, set to reveal its fourth-quarter results on Thursday, saw a decrease in its shares following Trump's announcement of the executive order targeting Wall Street institutions' acquisitions of single-family homes. Despite efforts by the company to clarify its limited exposure and minimal impact on the American single-family housing market, concerns remain. Blackstone has emphasized that institutional ownership makes up only a small portion of the market at 0.5%. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ExxonMobil Q4 earnings preview: Refining helps offset lower oil prices. Expected EPS $1.68, revenue $82.28B, with strong refining performance. CEO open to re-entry in Venezuela under Trump.
data.description: ExxonMobil is set to report fourth-quarter earnings amidst softening crude prices and geopolitical uncertainty. Analysts predict EPS of $1.68 on revenue of around $82.28 billion, after a strong third quarter with $7.5 billion in earnings. The company's refining operations and record production in Guyana and the Permian Basin are expected to offset lower upstream realizations. Despite concerns about declining margins, ExxonMobil's integrated model is seen as resilient. CEO Josh Brown views the stock's recent performance as a "breakout" and highlights its value as a portfolio hedge against inflation. ExxonMobil's structural cost savings exceeding $14 billion since 2019 have been a key driver of its earnings.

data.title: TECregen appoints Dr. Klaas P. Zuideveld as CEO to lead thymus regeneration platform post CHF 10M seed financing.
data.description: Following a successful seed financing of CHF 10 million and expansion of the Board leadership, TECregen, a biotechnology firm based in Basel, Switzerland, revealed the appointment of Dr. Klaas P. Zuideveld, Ph.D., as the new Chief Executive Officer on January 29, 2026. Dr. Zuideveld's leadership aims to drive forward the company's pioneering work in thymus rejuvenation.

data.title: Biocomposites launches SYNICEM™ spacers in Canada, including hip, knee, and shoulder revisions. Various designs available.
data.description: Biocomposites, a global medical device company, has introduced preformed antibiotic loaded spacers for hip, knee, and shoulder revisions. These include the Charnley and Müller designs for hip spacers, and options for both left and right knee geometries. The company, based in KEELE, England, made this announcement on January 29, 2026.

data.title: ViCentra names Reman McDonagh as VP Global Marketing, brings 20+ years of diabetes experience for Kaleido launch.
data.description: An experienced marketing executive with over two decades of international experience in the diabetes sector has joined ViCentra in support of the European expansion and upcoming launch of the next-generation Kaleido insulin patch pump. The company, based in Utrecht, Netherlands, is gearing up for this significant move with the new executive bringing a wealth of knowledge to the team from various global diabetes roles. The announcement was made on January 29, 2026, marking a key step in ViCentra's strategy for growth and innovation in the medical device market.
